Notice Of Defendants Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs SECURITY NATIONAL GUARANTY, INC., and EDMOND GHANDOUR's Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint is granted. The court "shall grant" leave to file a cross-complaint "at any time during the course of the action." (Code Civ. Proc. 426.50; see Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99 ["A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result."].) Plaintiff Medley Capital Corporation has not shown that Defendants are acting in bad faith, have unduly delayed in bringing their motion, or that it would be prejudiced by allowing the cross-complaint.

Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Cohen of Joseph & Cohen telephone number 415-817-9200 for Plaintiff.
Marc Adams of Jeffer Mangles Butler & Mitchell LLP telephone number 949-623-7230 for Defendants.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SECURITY NATIONAL GUARANTY, INC. and EDMOND GHANDOUR'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied. The parties agree that defendants have met the first prong of the anti-SLAPP statute. A claim for malicious prosecution necessarily implicates petitioning activity. Plaintiff demonstrates a probability of prevailing on its malicious prosecution claim. The elements of the cause of action are: (1) favorable termination in favor of plaintiff on the merits; (2) lack of probable cause; and (3) malice. In Contemporary Services Corp. v. Staff Pro Inc. (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 1043, 1056-1057, the court explains that the termination must reflect on the merits of the action and the plaintiff's innocence of the misconduct alleged in the lawsuit. In the underlying case and in this case, Mr. Ghandour asserts that the parties entered into the discount payment agreement or "DPO" on 6/27/13. (Ghandour dec., pars. 15 & 17). Plaintiff presents numerous e-mails from Mr. Ghandour that show that no such oral agreement existed. The e-mails that occurred subsequent to the 6/27/13 meeting show that Mr. Ghandour knew that there was "no definitive agreement." (Fredericks Dec., Ex. M; 10/8/13 e-mail.) These e-mails show that the fraud claim was baseless and indicate plaintiff's innocence. These same e-mails show that defendant's cross-complaint lacked probable cause. The claim was not legally tenable. Plaintiff presents sufficient evidence to demonstrate malice as the trier of fact could infer that the cross-complaint was filed and maintained for an improper purpose. See Sierra Club Foundation v. Graham (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 1135, 1156-1157. Lastly, plaintiff can establish damages as it expended fees to defend the cross-complaint. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Stay Of Discovery Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 425.16(G);
Off Calendar Dec-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JONATHAN M. COHEN, TEL. NO. 1-415-817-9200 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARK S. ADAMS OOF THE JEFFER MONGELS BUTLER & MITCHELL LLP, TEL. NO. 1-949-623-7230 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - JUDGE KAHN DISCLOSED THAT HE WAS THE JUDGE ASSIGNED TO HEAR THE WRIT THAT THE SECURITY NATIONAL GUARANTY INC. FILED CONTESTING THE DENIAL BY THE CALIFORNIA COASTAL COMMISSION OF THE HOTEL PERMIT IN SAND CITY. THIS DOES NOT AFFECT HIS ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS PLAINTIFF MEDLEY CAPITAL CORPORATION'S Motion To Lift Stay Of Discovery Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 425.16(G). Plaintiff's motion to lift stay of discovery is denied. Plaintiff has not shown good cause to lift the discovery stay. Plaintiff does not satisfactorily explain what information it expects to receive from its requested discovery that would enable it to present a prima facie case. Moreover, plaintiff's discovery requests go well beyond the issues raised by the anti-SLAPP motion to dismiss. Lifting the discovery stay as requested by plaintiff would undermine, not further, the intent of the anti-SLAPP legislation. Defendant may not add new facts in the reply brief. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489
